mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals strengthening demand, which drives corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns higher over the subsequent 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth shows a negative correlation with SPX 20-day forward returns (rank IC <= 0) over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate by more than 0.2 percentage points over a three-month period signals labor market weakening, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in episodes where UNRATE triggers, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline year-over-year by more than 5% and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains above 5%, the combination signals a severe housing downturn compounded by tight monetary conditions, which depresses economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with housing stress
failure_condition: If in the 60 trading days following triggers, the S&P 500 returns are positive in at least 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test alpha 0.1), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles when the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, further increases signal excessive contraction that harms equity valuation through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This signal is only active in a tightening regime.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% and the central bank is hiking (previous value lower), the 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect robust manufacturing and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter. This is a persistent trend signal independent of regime.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth has a ranking IC lower than 0.05 for 60-day forward returns over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year growth in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 3% indicates overheating inflation, prompting tighter monetary policy that depresses equity prices, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months where CPI YoY > 3% the SPX 20-day return is positive more than half the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in payroll employment (PAYEMS rising) and low unemployment (UNRATE below 4.5%) signals a tight labor market that supports consumer income and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a short-term horizon of 5 trading days. Both conditions must be present for the signal to activate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth positive and UNRATE below 4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: If in months with both conditions met, the average 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p<0.1) or if the hit rate is below 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a deteriorating housing market regime, a decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.5 million units (annualized) signals deepening weakness in residential investment and consumer wealth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If when HOUST is below 1500, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p < 0.05), or if more than 40% of such periods have positive returns, the hypothesis is rejected.
